title: GLP-1
description: Glucagon-like peptide-1 (GLP-1) is an incretin hormone derived from the intestine that stimulates insulin secretion in a glucose-dependent manner while suppressing glucagon release. It acts on the brain to increase satiety, delays gastric emptying, and is primarily used to treat type 2 diabetes and obesity through GLP-1 receptor agonists.

research.title: GLP-1-based therapies for diabetes, obesity and beyond
research.summary: This review confirms GLP-1 therapies like semaglutide and tirzepatide are highly effective for glucose control and weight loss while reducing cardiovascular and renal mortality.

research.title: Hypoglycemic effect of C. butyricum-pMTL007-GLP-1 engineered probiotics on type 2 diabetes mellitus
research.summary: Engineered probiotics designed to continuously deliver GLP-1 successfully lowered blood glucose, improved islet cell function, and restored gut microbiota in diabetic models.

research.title: GDF8 and activin A blockade protects against GLP-1-induced muscle loss while enhancing fat loss
research.summary: GLP-1 receptor agonists can cause significant muscle loss as a side effect, but blocking myostatin and activin A can preserve muscle mass and optimize metabolic outcomes.

research.title: 2023 AHA/ACC/ACCP/ASPC/NLA/PCNA Guideline for the Management of Patients With Chronic Coronary Disease
research.summary: Clinical guidelines now formally recommend GLP-1 receptor agonists for patients with chronic coronary disease and type 2 diabetes to reduce major adverse cardiovascular events.

research.title: The role of glucagon-like peptide-1 in reproduction: from physiology to therapeutic perspective
research.summary: GLP-1 RAs show potential in managing reproductive health disorders like PCOS by restoring menstrual frequency and improving metabolic health.

research.title: GLP-1 rescued gestational diabetes mellitus-induced suppression of fetal thalamus development
research.summary: Animal models suggest GLP-1 may protect fetal neurological development, specifically correcting impaired thalamocortical axon growth caused by maternal diabetes.

sentiment.summary: Users report transformative weight loss and high effectiveness for glycemic control, though they frequently discuss managing gastrointestinal side effects and the risk of muscle mass reduction.

effects.label: Rapid Weight Loss:
effects.description: Highly effective for obesity management, particularly when combined with lifestyle interventions.

effects.label: Chronic Management:
effects.description: Shows high long-term success for reversing prediabetes and managing type 2 diabetes.

effects.label: Metabolic Health:
effects.description: Effectively improves dyslipidemia and markers of inflammation like IL-6 and TNF-alpha.

effects.label: PCOS Regulation:
effects.description: Emerging utility in restoring menstrual cycles and managing insulin resistance in adolescents with PCOS.

dosage.label: Titration Schedule:
dosage.description: Commonly started at a low 'induction' dose (e.g., 0.25mg) to minimize nausea, increasing every 4 weeks.

dosage.label: Short-acting vs Long-acting:
dosage.description: Short-acting analogs may be used to specifically target postprandial glucose, while long-acting versions (weekly) are preferred for weight loss.

dosage.label: Engineered Probiotics:
dosage.description: Novel delivery methods via C. butyricum or L. gasseri are being researched to bypass daily injections.

dosage.label: Nutraceutical Induction:
dosage.description: 200mg daily of Eriocitrin has been shown to naturally increase endogenous GLP-1 levels by 17%.

sideEffects.label: Gastrointestinal Distress:
sideEffects.description: Nausea, vomiting, and constipation are the most frequently reported user side effects.

sideEffects.label: Muscle Loss:
sideEffects.description: Loss of muscle mass is a significant concern, often requiring high protein intake and resistance training to mitigate.

sideEffects.label: Islet Cell Apoptosis:
sideEffects.description: In diseased states, GLP-1 therapies help retard islet cell apoptosis, which is a positive secondary effect.

sideEffects.label: Gallbladder Issues:
sideEffects.description: Rapid weight loss associated with GLP-1 can increase the risk of gallbladder-related complications.

availability.label: Prescription Requirement:
availability.description: GLP-1 receptor agonists (Semaglutide, Tirzepatide) are strictly prescription-only medications.

availability.label: Supply Shortages:
availability.description: Significant global demand has led to frequent sourcing challenges and intermittent availability for patients.
